behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is parser output handed to hook-input handling, not just isolated parsing. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process wiring is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model output or agent pipeline involved. Rejected higher level: behavior is about valid JSON + local handling semantics, not real process/OS effects, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test that feeds valid JSON with code fences/backticks/$vars through the real parser + hook input handler stack, and assert successful parse plus normal downstream handling state.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses module wiring and no external infra, matching Integration floor.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at parser-to-trace handoff (error object/context propagation), which is a module boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network requirement if trace sink is in-memory. MOCK-HIDE: a pure unit parser test could miss missing/incorrect trace context fields. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text requires malformed-input diagnostic trace content, not subprocess/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use malformed JSON fixtures in an in-process test over parser + trace-record creation path, assert `json_parse_failed` type and required diagnostic context fields pinpointing malformed section.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertions depend on cross-module error-context propagation, which a unit-only test may miss.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is policy orchestration from parse-failure signal to handled result + telemetry intent marker across local components. REAL-INFRA: explicitly local path; no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: disqualified by behavior’s local mapping focus, not runtime process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Drive a parse-failure outcome into the real error-policy module with telemetry-intent recorder stub/in-memory sink, assert non-throw handled result and telemetry-intent flag/record set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires local component interaction but not external infrastructure.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicitly requires real process execution and malformed stdin behavior, which crosses subprocess/OS boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes can miss actual stdin decoding, process lifecycle, and exit/flush ordering failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected lower levels: behavior text explicitly demands real process execution, so Unit/Integration are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the hook executable as a subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, assert process exits without crash, and verify expected trace signal is emitted before termination.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly exercises subprocess + stdin/exit semantics, matching System.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior requires real invocation path starting at stdin and ending at trace artifact emission; this is process/IO boundary testing. MOCK-HIDE: mocked stdin/sink may hide integration bugs in actual invocation wiring and artifact emission timing/format. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: disqualified because behavior explicitly calls for real invocation path, not only in-process module composition.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook via its real invocation entrypoint, provide malformed stdin, and verify produced trace artifact from the actual sink contains expected parse-failure signal.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Includes real stdin entrypoint and end-to-end IO artifact emission, requiring System.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: distinction relies on shared parsing + classification/trace behavior across multiple cases, not a single pure function in isolation. REAL-INFRA: can be validated in-process with fixture matrix; no OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior is semantic differentiation of inputs/errors, not real-process IO robustness.
behaviors.test_description: Add a parameterized in-process regression suite covering valid markdown-heavy JSON and malformed JSON fixtures through parser + failure-classification/trace path, asserting only malformed cases produce parse-failure attribution.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cross-module regression matrix is needed; external infrastructure is not.
